solution: 
```class Solution:
    def findRepeatedDnaSequences(self, s: str) -> List[str]:
        sequences = set()
        repeated = set()
        for i in range(len(s) - 9):
            seq = s[i:i+10]
            if seq in sequences:
                repeated.add(seq)
            sequences.add(seq)
        return list(repeated)
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses two sets to keep track of unique and repeated sequences. It iterates over the string, adding each 10-letter sequence to the set of unique sequences. If a sequence is already in the set of unique sequences, it is added to the set of repeated sequences. This approach has a high efficiency due to the use of sets for fast lookups and insertions.
